title: elaphoglossum macrophyllum var. schmalzii
intro: Elaphoglossum macrophyllum var. schmalzii, a lesser-known species of fern, is a type of epiphyte that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its chemical composition. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum macrophyllum var. schmalzii are said to include the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
